_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: The diagnosis itself is an indication for drainage
As a part of the treatment of underlying pathology (malignancy or infective focus as tuberculosis or pyogenic)

preauth.label: Detailed clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission

claim.label: Culture & sensitivity report of the drained pus/ fluid

ppd_preauth.q: Are Detailed Clinical notes available – all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including detailed pelvic examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission?
Did clinical examination and imaging confirm the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery/procedure?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: History, clinical findings, or investigations not suggestive of pyometra requiring drainage

Detailed clinical notes (including history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission) are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Ultrasound Abdomen/pelvis report is missing or not indicative of pyometra at pre-authorization
Culture & sensitivity report of the drained pus/fluid is missing at claim submission
Histopathological examination report is missing at claim submission
Detailed operative/procedure notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia equipment and anesthetist
clinical_key_pointers: Pyometra is the accumulation of purulent material in the uterine cavity. Its reported incidence is 0.01-0.5% in gynecologic patients; however, as far as elderly patients are concerned, its incidence is 13.6%.
The most common cause of pyometra: Malignant diseases of genital tract and the consequences of their treatment (radiotherapy).
Other causes are benign tumors: Leiomyoma, Endometrial polyps, Senile cervicitis, Cervical occlusion after surgery, Puerperal infections, Congenital cervical anomalies
surgical_options: The treatment of pyometra consists of: Dilating the cervix
Perform Dilatation & Curettage 1 – 2 weeks later to rule out carcinoma either of the body of the uterus or cervical canal
If the uterus gets perforated, immediate hysterectomy is indicated
